result.text:
I have enough grounding. Key findings:
- `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (build-runner.ts) confirms the bug: regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` extracts the **directory** name `<seg>` and returns it verbatim; `scopedCargoArgs` passes it as `-p <seg>`.
- The touched-set extraction is already complete (regex captures `workflows` from `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`) — so Fix 2 is mostly subsumed by Fix 1's dir→name mapping; the only requirement is correct conversion.
- **Clarification**: the Fix 3 prompt text lives in `src/prompts.ts` (`buildImplementPrompt`, QA prompt), NOT inline in `src/stages/implementation.ts`/`verify.ts` — those stages only *call* the builders. I'll flag this in ACs/open-questions.

Producing the structured requirements now.

control.title: Resolve real cargo package names in the scope-aware build-gate (and forbid --lib-only self-verification)
control.executiveSummary: The scope-aware build gate in src/build-runner.ts derives cargo `-p` flags from workspace DIRECTORY names (e.g. `crates/data/` → `data`) instead of real package names (e.g. `stockfan-data`), so `cargo build -p data` fails instantly with "package ID specification `data` did not match any packages" on any workspace that prefixes crate names — false-failing every gate attempt and aborting the run before anything compiles. Fix 1 adds a `cargo metadata`-backed `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` that maps dir→real package `name` (with a documented dir-name fallback and a never-throw + per-cwd cache), wired into `detectTouchedCargoPackages`; Fix 2 confirms the already-complete touched-set extraction now yields correct names; Fix 3 strengthens the implementation/QA prompt builders (in src/prompts.ts, invoked by src/stages/implementation.ts and verify.ts) to forbid `--lib`-only verification and require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` plus spec-mandated e2e/integration targets.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: src/build-runner.ts exports `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]` that runs `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml` (bounded by the existing timeout envelope, via discrete argv `spawnSync`, no `shell:true`), parses the JSON, and maps each touched `crates/<dir>/` segment to the workspace package whose `manifest_path` parent directory equals `crates/<dir>/` (compare the directory CONTAINING the package's Cargo.toml, supporting manifest-in-subdir), returning the real package `name`s deduped and in first-seen order.

AC-02 — Never-throw + fallback chain: the metadata helper is wrapped in a defensive try/catch that NEVER throws; on spawn error / non-zero exit / timeout / missing cargo / no match, it falls back to returning the input touched directory names verbatim (identity), so workspaces where dir==name and non-cargo dirs degrade to today's behavior identically. The fallback is documented in a JSDoc comment.

AC-03 — Per-cwd in-memory cache: a module-level `Map<cwd, MetadataResult>` memoizes the parsed `cargo metadata` output so a single gate run that calls the resolver multiple times spawns `cargo metadata` at most once per cwd. The cache is process-local (cleared on process exit; never persisted across runs to avoid stale results).

AC-04 — `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?)` keeps its existing `git diff --name-only` spawn + regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` + dedupe logic, but passes the collected directory segments through `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the FINAL step so it returns REAL package names; on the stockfan shape (dirs data/tools/workflows) it returns `['stockfan-data','stockfan-tools','stockfan-workflows']`, producing `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` end-to-end via the unchanged `scopedCargoArgs`/`scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs`.

AC-05 — Complete touched set: a touched `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` makes `stockfan-workflows` appear in the resolved scope (regex already captures the `workflows` segment; this AC asserts the dir→name mapping covers it, so spec-mandated integration/e2e tests in a touched crate run via `cargo test -p stockfan-workflows`).

AC-06 — New unit tests (hermetic, `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked) covering: (a) dir→name resolution against a stubbed `cargo metadata` JSON with prefixed names + a manifest-in-subdir package; (b) metadata failure (spawn error / non-zero exit / timeout) falls back to the dir-name identity list; (c) a cache-hit scenario where the metadata spawn mock is called only once across two resolver calls for the same cwd; (d) an end-to-end workspace where dir != name produces correct `-p` flags through `scopedCargoArgs`.

AC-07 — Fix 3 (prompt-level): the implementation/verify self-verification prompt builders (the authoritative text lives in src/prompts.ts — `buildImplementPrompt` and the QA/verify prompt invoked by src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts) are strengthened to REQUIRE `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` flag (so integration binaries under `tests/` run), PLUS any spec-mandated e2e/integration target, and to include an explicit instruction: do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence; `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries. This is a prompt-text change only — no control-flow or nodes/workflow/pipeline change.

AC-08 — No regression: all existing scope-aware tests pass unchanged (build-runner-touched-crates, build-runner-autoscope, build-runner-inscope-classification, build-runner-scoped-args, build-runner-packages, build-runner-nonregression, build-runner-timeout, build-runner-docs); non-cargo repos (go/python/node/mixed) and non-git dirs remain byte-identical (the metadata tier runs only when `language === 'rust'` and a non-empty scope resolves, exactly as today).

AC-09 — Theme method-binding preserved: any new rendering uses method-style `theme.fg(...)` (or a wrapper `const fg=(c,t)=>theme.fg(c,t)`); NEVER destructures `theme.fg`. No new theme tokens are introduced and the rendering layer is untouched. (tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts must still pass.)

AC-10 — `npm run typecheck` passes (strict-clean) and `npm test` passes (existing + new) with no new runtime dependencies; the only new spawned process is `cargo metadata --no-deps --manifest-path ...`, cached per cwd per run.

nonFunctional: Performance: `cargo metadata --no-deps` is the single new spawned process, bounded by the existing timeout envelope, and memoized per cwd so a gate run spawns it at most once; cache is process-local (never persisted) to avoid staleness across runs.

Reliability / fault tolerance: the metadata resolver and the gate itself NEVER throw — every failure path (spawn error, non-zero exit, timeout, missing cargo, malformed JSON, no matching package) degrades gracefully to the documented dir-name identity fallback, preserving today's safe `[]`/workspace-wide semantics; a non-cargo or non-git repo behaves identically to before.

Correctness / no-false-green: Fix 3 is prompt-only and must not change control flow; the `--lib` prohibition removes a vacuous-green vector (declaring success on unit tests only) without weakening the deterministic gate's out-of-scope classifier (which still never grants a false green).

Backward compatibility: dir==name workspaces get an identity mapping (no-op), so existing tests and real dir==name monorepos are byte-identical; the scoped-vs-workspace-wide gate STRATEGY is unchanged (touched-crate scoping remains default).

Security: package names flow as discrete argv elements through `spawnSync` with no `shell:true` (matching the existing spawn pattern in detectTouchedCargoPackages/runBuildGate), so no metadata-derived string ever reaches a shell.

TypeScript strict-clean + zero new runtime dependencies; changes confined to src/build-runner.ts (Fix 1+2) and prompt text in src/prompts.ts (Fix 3) plus new tests — nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts and the rendering layer are untouched.
openQuestions: Fix 3 location: the authoritative prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts (buildImplementPrompt + the QA/verify prompt), which the stages merely call — not inline in src/stages/implementation.ts/verify.ts as the task brief implied. Confirm the `--lib` prohibition should be added in src/prompts.ts (recommended: it is the single source of truth the stages consume) rather than re-stated in each stage.

Fix 2 scope: the existing regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` ALREADY captures every touched crate segment (including `workflows` from `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`), so no extraction fix is required — Fix 2 is fully delivered by Fix 1's dir→name conversion. Confirm there is no separate single-crate edge case to handle (e.g. root-level workspace Cargo.toml edits touching ALL packages).

Should `resolveCargoPackageNames` also be exported for unit testing even if `detectTouchedCargoPackages` is the only internal caller? (Recommended: yes, export it so AC-06 tests can target it directly without going through git diff.)
Cache invalidation: confirm a process-local Map is acceptable (recommended: yes — a gate run is a single process and cross-run staleness is the real risk); no TTL or fs-based cache is needed.

Metadata matching granularity: confirm matching on the directory CONTAINING the package's Cargo.toml (`manifest_path` parent dir) vs the package `name` itself — recommended to match directory, since the bug is precisely that directory != name and the mapping must bridge them.

result.text: Confirmed. I now have the decisive evidence: `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` returns `packages[]` where each entry has `name` + `manifest_path` (absolute path to that package's Cargo.toml). Parent-dir of `manifest_path` = the package's directory — robustly mapping `crates/alpha/`→`stockfan-alpha` and `crates/prefixed-beta/`→`stockfan-beta` (exactly the stockfan bug shape). I also confirmed `workspace_members` are opaque `path+file://...#name@version` IDs (NOT usable as `-p` flags directly). That's enough — writing up.

control.title: Resolving real Cargo package names via `cargo metadata` for the scope-aware build gate
control.summary: The build gate bug is purely a dir-name vs package-name mismatch: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` maps `crates/<dir>/` paths to the DIRECTORY segment, but cargo's `-p` flag wants the package NAME declared in `Cargo.toml` (often prefixed, e.g. dir `data/`→package `stockfan-data`). The authoritative fix is `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, whose JSON `packages[]` array gives each workspace member a `name` AND an absolute `manifest_path`; the parent directory of `manifest_path` is the package's directory, so matching `crates/<dir>/` against that parent maps dir→real-name robustly even for nested/prefixed dirs. I empirically confirmed this schema (SRC-001): `crates/alpha`→`stockfan-alpha`, `crates/prefixed-beta`→`stockfan-beta` (the exact stockfan shape). A critical gotcha: top-level `workspace_members` are opaque `path+file://…#name@version` IDs that MUST NOT be parsed into `-p` names — go through `packages[]` instead. `--no-deps` keeps output small/fast (only workspace + local members, no registry/transitive deps); `--format-version 1` pins the stable schema so future format bumps don't break parsing. The metadata spawn reuses the repo's existing `spawnSync` discrete-argv + timeout-envelope pattern (no `shell:true`, so package/path data never reaches a shell — BP-001), is wrapped in never-throw try/catch with an identity dir-name fallback (BP-002, satisfies AC-02 + non-cargo/non-git unchanged), and is memoized per-cwd in a process-local `Map` (BP-003, AC-03) since one gate run may call it multiple times. The metadata tier only runs when `language==='rust'` and a non-empty scope resolves (existing guard unchanged — AC-08). Fix 3 is prompt-text-only in src/prompts.ts (confirmed present, ~22KB; the verify prompt at ~line 98 already says "Run the full test suite") — strengthen it to forbid `--lib`-only evidence and require `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec e2e.

options.name: A. Map dir→name via `packages[].manifest_path` parent-dir match (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Matches each touched `crates/<dir>/` to the package whose `manifest_path` parent-dir equals `crates/<dir>/` (normalize via path.relative against `workspace_root` or absolute compare). Empirically validated: handles dir==name (identity), prefixed dirs (`data`→`stockfan-data`), and manifest-in-subdir. Identity for non-cargo/non-matching dirs (safe fallback). Only real cost: a `cargo metadata` spawn per cwd (cached). This is what AC-01/04/06 mandate.

options.name: B. Parse `workspace_members` opaque IDs to extract `#name@version`
options.tradeoffs: Avoids scanning `packages[]`, but `workspace_members` entries are `path+file:///<abs>#<name>@<ver>` URLs — fragile to URL-encoding, the `path+file://` scheme, and format-version drift. Reinvents manifest_path matching anyway. Reject: not robust, no benefit over A.

options.name: C. `packages[]` filtered to `workspace_members` set, then dir-match
options.tradeoffs: Most strictly correct (excludes any locally-pathed dep that is NOT a workspace member but shares a `crates/` dir), marginally safer than A for exotic layouts. Cost: extra ID-set intersection code + more complexity. A is sufficient for the stockfan-class bug and all realistic workspaces; reserve C only if a transitive-dep-collision edge case appears.

options.name: D. Run metadata tier lazily only when `language==='rust'` and scope non-empty (RECOMMENDED placement)
options.tradeoffs: Reuses the existing guard so go/python/node/mixed repos and non-git dirs stay byte-identical (AC-08). `cargo metadata` is the ONLY new spawn, fires once per cargo-gate-run per cwd. Alternatives (eager/unconditional) waste a spawn on every non-rust repo and break the non-cargo-unchanged AC.

options.name: E. Per-cwd module-level `Map<cwd, MetadataResult>`, process-local, never persisted (RECOMMENDED cache)
options.tradeoffs: One gate run may call the resolver multiple times; a process-local memo bounds spawns to ≤1 per cwd per run (AC-03). NEVER persist to disk across runs — stale results if Cargo.toml changes between runs is the explicit risk AC-03 forbids. Simplest correct option; no eviction logic needed for a short-lived gate process.

options.name: F. Fix 3 as prompt-text-only change in src/prompts.ts (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: The verify/implementation self-verification prompt is the single source of truth the agent reads; editing it (forbid `--lib`, require `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec e2e) is the minimal-blast-radius fix with zero control-flow/nodes/workflow/pipeline risk (AC-07). Alternatives (gate-level enforcement, parsing agent test output) violate the 'prompt-only' constraint and touch nodes.ts/workflow.ts — out of scope.

openIssues: Normalization of path comparison: `manifest_path` is absolute while touched dirs are relative `crates/<dir>/` segments. Implementer must normalize both sides (recommend `path.relative(metadata.workspace_root, path.dirname(pkg.manifest_path))` then compare to `crates/<dir>`, or absolute-join cwd+dir). Minor but a real implementation choice the spec leaves open.

Confirm `buildImplementPrompt` (not just the verify prompt at ~line 98) exists in src/prompts.ts and is the authoritative text both src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts read — AC-07 asserts this; I only confirmed the verify prompt exists. Quick grep resolves it during implementation, not a research blocker.

`cargo metadata` requires a valid workspace/manifest to load — a syntactically broken Cargo.toml in the target repo yields non-zero exit → the never-throw fallback returns dir-name identity. This is acceptable (degrades to today's broken-on-broken-manifest behavior) but worth a JSDoc note that a half-broken manifest defeats the fix for THAT repo only.

Cross-drive / Windows path separators: `manifest_path` uses OS-native separators while the regex-derived touched segments are POSIX-style (`crates/data/`). On non-POSIX hosts the dir match could miss; recommend normalizing to POSIX (`path.posix`) or splitting on both `/` and `\\`. Low-risk since CI is Linux but worth a defensive normalize.

result.text:
I have all the evidence I need. All 4 prior open issues resolve cleanly from the code + the authoritative `cargo metadata` schema. Writing the structured brief now.

options.name: Relativize to workspace_root, then split on both separators (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Compute `rel = path.relative(metadata.workspace_root, path.dirname(pkg.manifest_path))` then compare `rel.split(/[\\/]/)` against `["crates", touchedDir]`. PROS: resolves ISS-1 (absolute-vs-relative) and ISS-4 (Windows backslashes) in ONE step since splitting on `/` and `\` normalizes OS separators; robust to manifests nested deeper than `crates/<dir>/Cargo.toml` (manifest-in-subdir AC-06 case) because the workspace-relative prefix stays `crates/<dir>`; tolerant of symlinked worktrees (relative, not cwd-anchored); trivially de-dupable. CONS: depends on `workspace_root` being present and correct (guaranteed non-empty whenever `packages[]` is non-empty, so safe); one extra path op per package (negligible — packages count is small). Matches the SRC-001 schema where workspace_root is always absolute. This is the approach the prior research already leaned toward.

options.name: Absolute resolve of touched dir, compare dirnames
options.tradeoffs: Compute `path.resolve(cwd, "crates", touchedDir)` and compare === `path.dirname(pkg.manifest_path)`. PROS: simplest mental model; no dependency on workspace_root field. CONS: assumes `cwd === workspace_root` (true in this harness today, but fragile if the worktree is symlinked or run from a sub-path); fails the manifest-in-subdir AC-06 case (`crates/data/sub/Cargo.toml` would not equal `crates/data`); STILL needs its own POSIX normalize for ISS-4 (path.resolve produces `\` on Windows), so it does not collapse ISS-1+ISS-4. options.name: basename-only match (NOT recommended)
options.tradeoffs: Compare `path.basename(path.dirname(manifest_path)) === touchedDir`. PROS: trivially short. CONS: false positives across sibling crates sharing a final directory name at different nesting depths; definitively fails the manifest-in-subdir AC-06 case; does not honor the `crates/<dir>/` prefix contract the rest of the module relies on. control.title: Resolving Real Cargo Package Names via `cargo metadata` for the Scope-Aware Build Gate
control.summary: The build gate currently maps touched `crates/<dir>/` paths to their directory name and passes that as `cargo build -p <dir>`, which cargo rejects instantly whenever a workspace prefixes crate names (dir `data` → package `stockfan-data`), false-failing every gate. The robust fix is to resolve dir→real-name through `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, which emits a JSON array of workspace members each carrying `name`, `manifest_path`, and a `workspace_root` field. The canonical mapping is: relativize each package's `manifest_path` to `workspace_root`, then compare the parent directory of that relative manifest against each touched `crates/<dir>/` segment using a POSIX/Windows-agnostic split (`/[\\/]/`). This single approach collapses two prior open issues (absolute-path mismatch and Windows separators). Because `cargo metadata` is a spawn (not free), it must be memoized per-cwd for the lifetime of one gate run. The entire resolver must be wrapped in a never-throw try/catch that degrades to the existing dir-name identity list (so `dir==name` workspaces, non-cargo dirs, and a syntactically broken manifest all behave identically to today). The implementation/verify self-check prompt (`buildImplementPrompt`, and defensively `buildQaPrompt`) must be strengthened to forbid declaring green on `--lib`-only evidence, since `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries and the e2e crate. All open issues from the prior research report are resolved; the only code-task residual is a JSDoc note on the new helper documenting the broken-manifest degradation.

options.name: Option 1 (RECOMMENDED, RESOLVED): Relativize `manifest_path` to `workspace_root`, then split on /[\\/]/ for dir→name match
options.tradeoffs: Maps a touched dir to the workspace member whose Cargo.toml lives at that dir (handles manifest-in-subdir), and collapses ISS-1 (absolute vs relative) + ISS-4 (Windows `\` vs POSIX `/`) into one robust comparison [SRC-01]. Pros: correct on prefixed names, no extra spawns beyond one cached `cargo metadata`, no new deps, identity fallback keeps `dir==name`/non-cargo unchanged [SRC-02]. Cons: relies on `workspace_root` being present (guaranteed by `--format-version 1`); a half-broken target manifest makes `cargo metadata` exit non-zero, which the never-throw fallback converts to dir-name identity — accepted degradation for THAT repo only (document in JSDoc) [ISS-3].

options.name: Option 2: Compare full absolute `manifest_path` parent against each touched dir
options.tradeoffs: Requires touching paths to be made absolute (join with cwd) and is fragile across path forms (Windows separators, symlinks). Rejected because it does not collapse ISS-1/ISS-4 and adds normalization surface area for no benefit over Option 1.

options.name: Option 3: Parse each crate's `Cargo.toml` directly for `[package] name = "..."`
options.tradeoffs: Would spawn/parse one file per touched dir (or do a naive regex on multiple Cargo.toml), is more spawns and more fragile (TOML edge cases, workspace vs member manifests) than one cached `cargo metadata`. Rejected — `cargo metadata` is the single source of truth cargo itself uses.

options.name: Fix 3 (prompt-only): Strengthen `buildImplementPrompt` (+ `buildQaPrompt`) to forbid `--lib`-only verification
options.tradeoffs: Authoritative target confirmed at src/prompts.ts:93, imported by src/stages/implementation.ts:10 and used at :73; verify.ts does NOT currently import buildQaPrompt, so strengthen it defensively too [ISS-2]. Pros: cheapest possible fix for the `--lib`-green false-success (run log shows the agent declared success after `cargo test -p stockfan-data --lib --quiet`, skipping integration binaries + e2e). Cons: prompt-level only, so it depends on the agent honoring the instruction — but that matches the existing contract (framework gate catches the objective failure; prompt prevents the subjective mis-green). No control-flow change.

openIssues: No research blockers remain. All four prior open issues are resolved: ISS-1 (absolute manifest_path vs relative touched dirs) and ISS-4 (Windows \ vs POSIX / separators) are collapsed by Option 1 (relativize to workspace_root, split on /[\\/]/); ISS-2 (confirm buildImplementPrompt is authoritative) is confirmed at src/prompts.ts:93 → src/stages/implementation.ts:10/:73; ISS-3 (broken Cargo.toml defeats the fix for that one repo) is ACCEPTED DEGRADATION (cargo metadata exits non-zero, never-throw fallback returns dir-name identity = today's behavior).

The repair loop re-runs the identical framework-derived command (3 attempts + pre-merge, all byte-identical because the framework, not the agent, owns the `-p` flags); every attempt fails identically; the run aborts.

MINIMAL deterministic repro (no cargo/git needed): assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs(['data'])` deep-equals `['cargo','build','-p','data','--quiet']` — proving the gate passes the raw directory name straight through with no metadata resolution. The fix flips this to `-p stockfan-data` once `resolveCargoPackageNames` is wired in.

The target of this fix is `src/build-runner.ts`, the "deterministic build/test/typecheck gate — the HARD test oracle" that spawns real cargo/git commands and is consumed by Stages 9/9.2/11. The confirmed bug: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:161-181) returns workspace DIRECTORY names (`crates/<seg>/` → `<seg>`), which `scopedCargoArgs` (src/build-runner.ts:213-228) passes verbatim as `-p <name>`; cargo rejects these on any workspace that prefixes crate names. Fix 1 adds a `cargo metadata --no-deps` resolver mapping dir→real-package-name; Fix 2 verifies the full touched set survives; Fix 3 is a prompt change. The verify-loop should NOT attempt to bring up a service; gate green is determined solely by `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and `npm test` (vitest run) both passing.

control.title: Cargo Package Name Resolution for the Scope-Aware Build Gate
control.summary: Fix the scope-aware cargo build-gate in this TypeScript pi-extension so it resolves REAL cargo package names from `cargo metadata` instead of workspace DIRECTORY names. Today `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts) runs `git diff --name-only`, maps each `crates/<seg>/` path to its directory segment, and returns those segments verbatim. `scopedCargoArgs` then emits `-p <dir>`. On any prefixed-crate workspace (stockfan: dirs `data/tools/workflows` → packages `stockfan-data/stockfan-tools/stockfan-workflows`) the gate runs `cargo build -p data -p tools --quiet`, cargo rejects it with `package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages` (exit 101), and the run false-fails before compiling anything — the repair loop cannot fix a framework-derived command. The fix adds `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` (spawns cached `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml`, maps each touched dir to the workspace package whose `manifest_path` parent's first `crates/<seg>/` segment matches, never throws, identity-falls-back to directory names, memoized per-cwd), wires it as the FINAL mapping step inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, guarantees the complete touched set (e2e crate `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` → `stockfan-workflows` is included), and strengthens `buildImplementPrompt` + `buildQaPrompt` (src/prompts.ts) to forbid `--lib`-only self-verification and require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec-mandated e2e. Pure TS change: only src/build-runner.ts, src/prompts.ts, and new/updated tests. nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts/render/theme are untouched. Backward compatible: dir==name workspaces, non-cargo repos, and non-git dirs are byte-identical to today (metadata tier runs only when `language==='rust'` and a non-empty scope resolves). No new runtime deps; the only new spawned process is cached `cargo metadata --no-deps`.
control.architecture:
The change is localized to two source files and a new test file, structured as a four-phase DAG where Phase 3 (prompt discipline) is fully parallelizable with Phases 1–2 (resolver + wiring) because it touches no shared code.

INPUTS / CONTRACTS (all signatures frozen):
- `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd: string, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]` (NEW, exported from src/build-runner.ts). Pure-mapping over a cached side-effect. Given touched DIRECTORY segments (e.g. `["data","tools","workflows"]`), returns REAL package names (e.g. `["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows"]`), deduped, first-seen order preserved.
- `loadCargoMetadata(cwd: string): CargoMetadataResult` (NEW, private). Side-effecting spawn, memoized. `CargoMetadataResult = { ok: true; packages: Array<{ name: string; manifestDir: string }> } | { ok: false }`.
- `cargoMetadataCache: Map<string, CargoMetadataResult>` (NEW, module-level, process-local). Keyed by absolute `cwd`. Stores either the parsed package list OR a `{ ok:false }` failure sentinel so a failing/missing `cargo` is not re-spawned within one run. Never persisted (process exit clears it) → no stale results across runs (SCENARIO-006).
- `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd: string, baseRef?: string): string[]` (MODIFIED). Signature unchanged. Internally unchanged: `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` discrete-argv spawn (no `shell:true`), regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` (first `crates/<seg>/` segment wins), `dedupePreservingOrder`. ONLY addition: the deduped directory segments are passed through `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the FINAL step before return.
- `scopedCargoArgs`, `scopedCargoBuildArgs`, `scopedCargoTestArgs`, `scopedCargoClippyArgs`, `classifyOutOfScopeErrors`, `runBuildGate` (UNCHANGED). `runBuildGate`'s FOUR-tier precedence (opts.testPackages > SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES > detectTouchedCargoPackages > workspace-wide) is untouched — because the resolver is wired INSIDE `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, real names flow through every tier unchanged.

FIX 1 — METADATA RESOLUTION + CACHE (Phase 1):
`loadCargoMetadata(cwd)` spawns `spawnSync("cargo", ["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps","--manifest-path", join(cwd,"Cargo.toml")], { encoding:"utf8", timeout: resolveTimeoutMs() })` — discrete argv, no `shell:true`, same timeout envelope as the rest of the module (AC-10: only new spawn is this one). The `--manifest-path` points at the WORKSPACE-ROOT `Cargo.toml`; cargo returns ALL workspace members in `packages[]`, each carrying its individual `manifest_path`. The helper maps each to `{ name: pkg.name, manifestDir: dirname(pkg.manifest_path) }` and caches it. The whole body is wrapped in a defensive try/catch: spawn error (`r.error`), non-zero exit, timeout, missing `cargo`, JSON parse failure, or unexpected shape → cache `{ ok:false }` and return it. NEVER throws (AC-02, SCENARIO-003).

`resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` reads `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)`. If `ok:false` → return `dedupePreservingOrder(touchedDirs)` (identity fallback, AC-02). If `ok:true` → for each touched segment `d`, apply the SAME `crates/<seg>/` regex to each package's `manifestDir` and select the package whose first `crates/<seg>/` segment EQUALS `d`. This matching rule is what makes manifest-in-subdir work (SCENARIO-002): a package whose `Cargo.toml` lives at `crates/data/inner/Cargo.toml` has `manifestDir = ".../crates/data/inner"` whose first `crates/<seg>/` segment is `data`, so it matches touched segment `data` and resolves to its real `name`. Per-element identity fallback: a touched segment with no matching package degrades to its own directory name (SCENARIO-004). Result is deduped, first-seen order preserved. The fallback chain + never-throw contract is documented in a JSDoc comment (AC-02).

FIX 1 WIRING + FIX 2 — COMPLETE TOUCHED SET (Phase 2):
Inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, the existing `dedupePreservingOrder(pkgs)` call now feeds `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, deduped)` and that result is returned. Because the resolver is identity for dir==name and identity-on-failure, non-cargo/non-git/dir==name paths are byte-identical to today (AC-08). The existing regex already captures the `workflows` segment for `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`, so Fix 2 is verified (not rewritten): confirm extraction includes data, tools, AND workflows, then the resolver maps `workflows` → `stockfan-workflows` so spec-mandated e2e runs via `cargo test -p stockfan-workflows` (AC-05, SCENARIO-009). End-to-end, `scopedCargoBuildArgs(["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows"])` → `["cargo","build","-p","stockfan-data","-p","stockfan-tools","-p","stockfan-workflows","--quiet"]` (AC-04, SCENARIO-007).

FIX 3 — PROMPT SELF-VERIFY DISCIPLINE (Phase 3, parallelizable):
The authoritative prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts. `buildImplementPrompt`'s "## Instructions" array and `buildQaPrompt`'s "## Instructions" array each get an appended, language-scoped instruction: "When verifying a Rust crate, run `cargo test -p <pkg>` WITHOUT `--lib` so the integration binaries under `tests/` execute, PLUS any spec-mandated e2e/integration target. Do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence — `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries." This is prompt-TEXT only; src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged, so there is no control-flow, nodes, workflow, or pipeline change (AC-07, SCENARIO-010/011). The phrasing is generic ("when Rust") so non-rust stacks are unaffected.

REGRESSION GUARDRAILS:
- The metadata tier runs ONLY when `runBuildGate`'s tier-3 path executes (rust repo, no higher-tier override) — identical to today's `detectTouchedCargoPackages` invocation site. Non-rust stacks never spawn `cargo metadata`.
- Theme method-binding preserved (AC-09, SCENARIO-015/024): no new rendering is introduced; if any were, it MUST use method-style `theme.fg(...)` or a wrapper `const fg=(c,t)=>theme.fg(c,t)` — NEVER `const fg = theme.fg` (detaches `this`, throws "reading 'fgColors'"). tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts stays green.

FILE INVENTORY:
- MODIFY: src/build-runner.ts — add `CargoMetadataResult` type, `cargoMetadataCache`, `loadCargoMetadata`, `resolveCargoPackageNames`; wire final mapping into `detectTouchedCargoPackages`.
- MODIFY: src/prompts.ts — append rust self-verify discipline to `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt`.
- CREATE: test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts — hermetic unit tests (AC-06) with `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked.
- NO CHANGE: src/stages/implementation.ts, src/stages/verify.ts (consume prompts unchanged), nodes.ts, workflow.ts, pipeline.ts, src/render/*, theme, package.json (no new deps).

DATA FLOW (single gate run): git diff → `crates/<dir>/` segments → dedupe → `resolveCargoPackageNames` (cache check → `cargo metadata` if cold → manifestDir-segment match → per-element identity fallback) → real package names → `scopedCargo{Build,Test,Clippy}Args` → `runBuildGate` exec → `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` (now classifies against REAL names, so in-scope partitioning is correct).
control.testingStrategy:
HERMETIC UNIT TESTS (AC-06, SCENARIO-016) in a NEW file test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts, with `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked via `vi.mock`/module replacement so no real `cargo`/`git` is spawned:

(a) DIR→NAME RESOLUTION: stub `spawnSync` to return a `cargo metadata` JSON whose `packages[]` carry prefixed names (`stockfan-data`, `stockfan-tools`, `stockfan-workflows`) AND one manifest-in-subdir package (`crates/data/inner/Cargo.toml`). Assert `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, ["data","tools","workflows","inner"])` returns `["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows", <inner-pkg-name>]` (AC-01, SCENARIO-001/002/017).
(b) FAILURE FALLBACK: make the mock throw / return `status:101` / emulate timeout (`error` with `killed`/`signal`) / return malformed JSON. For each, assert `resolveCargoPackageNames` returns the input directory names verbatim (identity), and NEVER throws (AC-02, SCENARIO-003/004/018/019). Also assert a touched dir with no matching package falls back to its own name.
(c) CACHE HIT: assert the spawn mock is invoked EXACTLY ONCE across two `resolveCargoPackageNames` calls for the same cwd (record call count on the mock) (AC-03, SCENARIO-005/020).
(d) END-TO-END -p FLAGS: build a workspace where dir≠name and assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs` over the resolved set yield `["cargo","build","-p","stockfan-data","-p","stockfan-tools","-p","stockfan-workflows","--quiet"]` and the matching test/clippy argv (AC-04, SCENARIO-007/021/022).
(e) COMPLETE TOUCHED SET: feed a diff containing `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_smoke.rs` and assert `stockfan-workflows` appears in the resolved scope (AC-05, SCENARIO-009).
(f) PROMPT DISCIPLINE: assert `buildImplementPrompt(...)` and `buildQaPrompt(...)` output strings CONTAIN the substrings `cargo test -p`, `WITHOUT --lib` / `no --lib`, `tests/` integration binaries, and `--lib`-only … not … green (AC-07, SCENARIO-010/011).

REGRESSION SUITE (AC-08, SCENARIO-012/013/023) — run the EXISTING suite unchanged and assert all green: build-runner-touched-crates, build-runner-autoscope, build-runner-inscope-classification, build-runner-scoped-args, build-runner-packages, build-runner-nonregression, build-runner-timeout, build-runner-docs, build-runner.test.ts. The key backward-compat invariants to assert inside/alongside these: (1) a dir==name workspace resolves identically (metadata is an identity no-op); (2) non-cargo repos (go/python/node/mixed) and non-git dirs are byte-identical (the metadata spawn is SKIPPED because `detectTouchedCargoPackages` returns `[]` for non-crate / non-git paths before any metadata call, and `runBuildGate` only enters the rust scoping tier when `language==='rust'`); (3) `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` now partitions against REAL names (a `stockfan-data` failure is in-scope when `stockfan-data` is scoped, where previously the `data` directory name never matched the real failure name).

THEME GUARDRAIL (AC-09, SCENARIO-015/024): run tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts; confirm no new rendering was added and any hypothetical rendering uses method-style `theme.fg(...)` (never destructured).

GATE VERIFICATION (AC-10, SCENARIO-014): `npm run typecheck` (strict-clean) and `npm test` (existing + new) both green; `grep` package.json to confirm no new runtime dependency was added; confirm the only new spawned process in build-runner.ts is the cached `cargo metadata --no-deps --manifest-path ...`.

MANUAL SANITY (not a test, evidence-only): on a real prefixed-crate workspace, `detectTouchedCargoPackages` now prints `stockfan-data stockfan-tools stockfan-workflows` and `cargo build -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows --quiet` compiles instead of exiting 101.

OUT OF SCOPE for testing: changing the scoped-vs-workspace-wide gate STRATEGY, baseline-diff scoping, any render/theme token, control-flow in nodes/workflow/pipeline.

tasks.description: In src/build-runner.ts add the `CargoMetadataResult` type: `{ ok: true; packages: Array<{ name: string; manifestDir: string }> } | { ok: false }`.
Add module-level `const cargoMetadataCache = new Map<string, CargoMetadataResult>();` (process-local, never persisted — cleared on process exit).

Implement private `loadCargoMetadata(cwd): CargoMetadataResult`: cache check first; else `spawnSync("cargo", ["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps","--manifest-path", join(cwd,"Cargo.toml")], { encoding:"utf8", timeout: resolveTimeoutMs() })`; on `r.error || r.status!==0 || non-string stdout` → cache+return `{ok:false}`; else `JSON.parse`, validate `Array.isArray(packages)`, map to `{name, manifestDir: dirname(manifest_path)}`, cache+return `{ok:true,packages}`; whole body try/caught → `{ok:false}`, never throws.

Implement + export `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]`: read `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)`; if `ok:false` return `dedupePreservingOrder(touchedDirs)`; else for each touched segment `d`, find the package whose `manifestDir` first `crates/<seg>/` segment (regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//`) equals `d` and push `pkg.name`, else push `d` (per-element identity fallback); dedupe preserving first-seen order; return. Add JSDoc documenting the never-throw + identity-fallback chain (AC-02).

Create test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts with `spawnSync` mocked: (a) prefixed-name + manifest-in-subdir dir→name resolution; (b) spawn-error / status≠0 / timeout / malformed-JSON → identity fallback + never-throw; (c) cache-hit (spawn called once for two calls same cwd); (d) unmatched touched dir → own name.

result.text: I have sufficient grounding. Key verifications complete: all `build-runner.ts` exports match the spec; the 4-tier precedence, regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//`, `dedupePreservingOrder`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, and `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` env var are all confirmed; all 9 regression test files and `stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` exist. But two critical grounding defects surfaced: (1) the repo uses `tests/` (plural) with vitest globs `tests/**`+`src/**`, yet the spec says `test/`; (2) `buildQaPrompt` has zero call sites — it's dead code, falsifying the spec's wiring claim.

control.title: Spec Review — Cargo Package Name Resolution for the Scope-Aware Build Gate
control.summary: The spec is technically sharp and well-grounded for the resolver core (Phase 1–2): every cited symbol in src/build-runner.ts — detectTouchedCargoPackages, the `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` regex, dedupePreservingOrder, resolveTimeoutMs, scopedCargo{Args,Build,Test,Clippy}Args, classifyOutOfScopeErrors, runBuildGate, and the SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES four-tier precedence — is verified present and matches the described contract. The `cargo metadata --no-deps` design is sound (workspace members are always in packages[]; --no-deps only excludes non-workspace deps), the discrete-argv no-shell spawn is consistent with module conventions, and AC-01..AC-10 plus SCENARIO-001..024 are all mapped. However, two grounding defects block approval as-written. (1) CRITICAL: the spec's Fix 3 appends rust self-verify discipline to buildQaPrompt and asserts "src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged" — but grep confirms buildQaPrompt has ZERO call sites in src/ or tests/ (verify.ts imports buildCodeReviewPrompt/buildAdversarialReview/buildFixPrompt/buildApiTestPrompt/buildUiTestPrompt, never buildQaPrompt). Appending to it is a no-op; the QA/verify agent — exactly where the "no --lib-only green" rule matters most — never receives the instruction. The string-containment test (Testing Strategy item f) would pass while delivering false confidence, matching the repo's known "implemented-and-unit-tested-but-NOT-wired-in" failure mode (learned.md score:62). (2) HIGH: the spec says CREATE test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts, but the repo has NO test/ directory — vitest.config.ts globs only tests/** and src/**. A literal implementation creates a file vitest never discovers, so AC-06/AC-10 can false-green. Both are bounded, recoverable reroutes/renames; the resolver architecture itself is APPROVED-quality.

findings.title: buildQaPrompt is dead code (0 call sites) — Fix 3 QA-path discipline will never reach an agent
findings.detail: Architecture (Fix 3) and FILE INVENTORY claim: "buildImplementPrompt's and buildQaPrompt's '## Instructions' array each get an appended instruction" and "src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged." Grounding check: grep -rn buildQaPrompt across src/ and tests/ returns ONLY its definition at src/prompts.ts:97 — no importer. src/stages/verify.ts:15 imports buildCodeReviewPrompt, buildAdversarialPrompt, buildFixPrompt, buildApiTestPrompt, buildUiTestPrompt (NOT buildQaPrompt); src/stages/implementation.ts:10 imports buildTddPrompt, buildImplementPrompt, buildCommitPrompt, buildImplementationSummaryPrompt (NOT buildQaPrompt). Consequence: appending rust verify discipline to buildQaPrompt has zero runtime effect; the verify/QA agent never sees "do NOT declare green on --lib-only evidence." AC-07 / SCENARIO-010/011 are only half-satisfiable (implementer path works via buildImplementPrompt; QA path is hollow). Testing Strategy item (f) asserts the substring exists in buildQaPrompt output — that test passes while proving nothing about agent behavior. This is the exact "tested-but-not-wired-in" anti-pattern in learned.md (score:62). Recommendation: reroute the QA-path discipline to a prompt verify.ts actually consumes (buildFixPrompt is the implementer-driven fix entry; buildCodeReviewPrompt is the reviewer entry) OR delete buildQaPrompt and restate AC-07 as implementer-path-only. Do not leave the spec claiming buildQaPrompt is live.

findings.title: New test path test/ does not exist — vitest will not discover the file (AC-06/AC-10 false-green risk)
findings.detail: FILE INVENTORY: "CREATE: test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts" and Testing Strategy repeatedly references test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts. Grounding check: find . -type d -name test (excluding node_modules) returns nothing; the repo uses tests/ (plural). vitest.config.ts: include: ["tests/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts"]. A file created at test/... is outside both globs and will NOT run under `npm test`. If the implementer follows the spec literally, AC-06's hermetic unit tests never execute yet `npm test` stays green (AC-10) — false confidence in the resolver. Recommendation: change every occurrence to tests/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts to match the established convention.

findings.title: "Language-scoped" prompt append bypasses the existing languageInstructions mechanism and isn't actually scoped
findings.detail: Fix 3 calls the appended instruction "language-scoped," but buildQaPrompt(s,c,phase) has no language parameter and buildImplementPrompt already receives rust-specific text via specialist.languageInstructions (src/prompts.ts:94; sourced from loadLangProfile in src/helpers.ts:78). The spec instead hardcodes rust text into the generic builder, so it is emitted in EVERY prompt regardless of classified language; only the runtime phrasing ("when verifying a Rust crate") gates behavior. This couples rust knowledge into the wrong layer and duplicates the lang-profile channel. Recommendation: either route the discipline through the rust lang-profile (loadLangProfile) so it is truly language-scoped and reaches buildImplementPrompt via the existing li block, or drop the "language-scoped" wording and explicitly state the text is unconditionally present but runtime-conditional.

findings.title: cargoMetadataCache key normalization unspecified — risks redundant spawns and weakens AC-03 "exactly once"
findings.detail: Architecture states cargoMetadataCache is "Keyed by absolute cwd." But neither loadCargoMetadata nor resolveCargoPackageNames is specified to canonicalize the key (e.g., path.resolve(cwd) or fs.realpathSync). detectTouchedCargoPackages receives cwd as-is from runBuildGate(opts.cwd); if a caller passes a relative path, a trailing slash, or a symlink variant, cache keys diverge and cargo metadata is re-spawned — directly weakening AC-03 / SCENARIO-005 ("spawn invoked EXACTLY ONCE") and the no-re-spawn-on-failure sentinel guarantee. Recommendation: mandate key normalization (resolve(cwd)) in the loadCargoMetadata contract and add a cache-key-normalization assertion to test (c).

findings.title: Regression-suite reference to "build-runner.test.ts" is ambiguous — two such files exist
findings.detail: Testing Strategy / regression suite lists "build-runner.test.ts" alongside the build-runner-* files. Grounding check: TWO files match — src/build-runner.test.ts AND tests/build-runner.test.ts (both covered by vitest's include globs). The spec does not disambiguate which must stay green. Low impact (both will run regardless), but for a backward-compat assertion that hinges on byte-identical behavior the target should be explicit. Recommendation: name both paths explicitly in the regression list.
